name: tiger-skills
description: Harness engineering (outer loop) + Code quality (inner loop). Based on walkinglabs learn-harness-engineering course.

SessionStart.prompt: HARNESS CLOCK-IN: Before any other action, (1) read AGENTS.md, (2) read progress.md, (3) read feature_list.json, (4) review git log --oneline -5, (5) run ./init.sh. Announce understanding before proceeding. If ./init.sh fails, fix the harness before working on features.

PreToolUse.prompt: BOOTSTRAP GATE: Before writing or editing code files, verify the four harness files exist on disk: AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, and init.sh. If any are missing, create them first using the minimal-pack templates. Do not write application code until the bootstrap gate passes.

EXPLORE BEFORE CODE: Before writing new code, HAVE YOU: (1) searched the codebase for existing types? (2) checked for existing functions that do 80%+ of what you need? (3) read the relevant files in the module? (4) built a mental Type Inventory? Never write a function that duplicates an existing one. Never use Any when a real type exists.
PRE-COMMIT CHECK: Before git commit, confirm: (1) verification ran fresh this session, (2) zero failures in output, (3) evidence recorded in feature_list.json.
PRE-PUSH CHECK: Before git push, confirm: (1) ./init.sh passes, (2) progress.md updated, (3) feature_list.json accurate, (4) all work committed with descriptive messages.

Stop.prompt: HARNESS CLOCK-OUT: Exit checklist: (1) ./init.sh passes, (2) progress.md updated, (3) feature_list.json updated, (4) all work committed, (5) no debug artifacts, (6) no temp files, (7) standard startup works, (8) next session ready.
